events.title: Stormy Night at Dolly Sods
events.thought: The Navigator is calling this Day 1 of Attempt 2. I'm still counting it as Day 25.
events.photoCaption: Habitat module deployed at Red Creek Campground in Dolly Sods. Windy, overcast skies and dense forest surround the site.
photoNote: Report. Day 25. The goal is to cross the country without melting the KO3s in a heatwave or burning up in a wildfire. An even bet, at best.
Navigator programmed in a more southern route this time. Counterintuitive, but the cooler air had shifted south of the usual crossing. Works for me.
A cold front rolled in as I rolled out of Maryland. Rained most of the way to Dolly Sods — the water felt good!

The Navigator recalibrated the Stauns: 3.5 turns for 30 psi. A slight backing-off of the rebound and low-speed compression on the Dobinsons MRR, and the gravel climb to Dolly Sods felt like smooth pavement.
A stormy night at 4,000 feet in West Virginia. I'm exactly where I'm supposed to be. Good to be rolling again.
